analysis_description: Round-3 tapes (days 0–2): BS implied vol per VEV from mids vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid; vega at IV; level-1 bid–ask spread on vouchers; IV smile width and neighbor |ΔIV| gaps; sweep of neighbor IV caps vs whether both strikes in the low-IV and high-IV decile pairs pass the cap (adjacent in strike-sorted order). TTE: csv day 0/1/2 → DTE 8/7/6 at open per round3description; intraday dte_eff = (8−day)−(timestamp//100)/10000; T_years=dte_eff/365 (same as plot_iv_smile_round3). IV solver: bisection on [1e-5,15] with intrinsic bounds.
used_to_decide: Chose initial neighbor cap near mean neighbor gap (~0.065) and below p90 gap (~0.20); cap sweep shows low-decile pairs almost always pass typical caps while high-decile pairs rarely pass when gaps are strike-adjacent—motivated fading high-IV less via cap and testing looser caps in traders. Vega scale informed sizing (median vega ~127). Spread stats ground passive join-bid/offer placement inside the book.

analysis_description: Empirical parameter grid on live backtester: v1 (cap 0.12, q=8) vs v2 (cap 0.15, q=5) produced identical total_profit (-11); next iteration should change signal (e.g. IV vs pooled smile residual) not only cap/size.
used_to_decide: Stop expanding neighbor cap / shrinking size alone; keep v1 as parent per tie-break (simpler than v2).

analysis_description: Supplemental IV/Greek + microstructure analysis on full Round-3 tapes (days 0-2): evaluated decile tradability using spread and vega/spread filters versus neighbor-cap gating. TTE mapping unchanged from round3description + intraday winding (csv day 0/1/2 -> DTE 8/7/6; dte_eff=(8-day)-(timestamp//100)/10000; T=dte_eff/365).
used_to_decide: Found strong asymmetry: low-decile pair almost always neighbor-cap valid, but high-decile pair almost never neighbor-cap valid despite being tradable by spread/vega. Implemented asymmetric gate (v3/v4) and then touch-execution variant (v5). Backtests showed v3/v4 unchanged at -11 and v5 degraded to -594, so keep v1 as best and simpler parent.

analysis_description: Round-3 tapes: per timestamp (subsample 1/20) compute BS implied vol from VEV_*/extract mids, then quadratic OLS in m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) with T from round3 DTE+intraday winding; report distribution of (max-min) across strikes for raw IV vs polyfit residual. Output quantifies that residual range is an order of magnitude smaller than raw IV range (median ~0.03 vs ~0.38), so neighbor gating on residuals is on the same scale as 0.12 caps.
used_to_decide: Motivated ranking residual IV to isolate relative mispricings along the smile; v6 backtest improved to +1141 total (days 0-2) vs v1 at -11. v7 cap sweep 0.15 was identical to v6; keep v6 as parent (simpler constant).

analysis_description: Parameter grid on smile-residual neighbor cap guided by prior tape summary iv_smile_residual_vs_raw (median cross-strike residual range ~0.03, p90 ~0.047). Tighter caps (0.05, 0.08) are meant to only trade when adjacent-strike residual gaps are small vs typical smile width.
used_to_decide: v8 (cap 0.05) and v9 (0.08) both underperformed v6 (0.12) on R3 0-2: 134 and 546 vs 1141 -- cap was too strict relative to live neighbor-gaps after ranking, removing volume without improving edge. Remain on v6 as best parent; do not match cap to p90 without validating strike-adjacent residual gaps.

analysis_description: Greek check on historical mids: at subsampled timestamps, compute quadratic smile IV residual per strike; correlate absolute residual with Black–Scholes vega at fitted IV. Pool ~6k strike-points across days 0-2. Confirms |resid| is only weakly related to vega (corr about -0.065), so residual mispricing is not simply a high-vega artifact.
used_to_decide: v10 (ACTION_STRIDE=2) improved total PnL to 1858 vs v6 1141, consistent with more frequent re-ranking catching moving residual deciles. v11 (larger size) reverted to 1141 like v6; next parent v10. Trade-off: ~2x backtest wall time vs v6.

analysis_description: Subsampled tape analysis of smile residual magnitude by vega bucket (BS vega at implied vol) plus strategy sweep: tested whether weighting residual rank by vega improves signal quality. Method: BS IV from mids, quadratic residual in m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T), T from round3 DTE mapping with intraday winding.
used_to_decide: Residual magnitude is only modestly higher in high-vega bucket than low-vega bucket; vega-weighted rank (v12) underperformed heavily (258). Cap/size variant v13 reached 1200 but still below v10 (1858). Keep v10 as best parent; tie-break still favors simpler implementations among equals.

analysis_description: IV + underlying link: on subsampled timestamps (step 20), compute quadratic-smile RMS of per-strike IV residuals across the ten vouchers; take one-step change in that RMS and correlate with VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid log-return. Same T and IV conventions as v6. Output in delta_atm_rms_vs_extract_return.json.
used_to_decide: Stride=1 re-rank (v14) lifted PnL to 4666 vs v10 1858; v15 size bump was identical, so v14 is best+simplest. New analysis shows negative correlation about -0.16 between delta smile-RMS and extract return, suggesting fast residual reshape when S moves, motivating even faster stride.

analysis_description: Regime-switch analysis required for family13: scan EWMA RV minus ATM-IV thresholds/cooldowns and measure switch frequency plus short-horizon post-switch extract returns before selecting thresholds. Used thresholds/cooldowns {(0.015,10),(0.02,15),(0.03,20)} on sampled timestamps; then backtested v16/v17 under --match-trades worse with full output logs saved.
used_to_decide: This exact variation was not previously explored in this branch. Switch scan showed stable low switch counts (~6) for tested thresholds/cooldowns. However, v16/v17 regime traders performed poorly (42 and -19), far below current best v14=4666 and benchmark bar 800k. Keep v14 as active parent while documenting family13 pivot attempt and saved full logs/fills summaries.

analysis_description: Priority underlying-propagation analysis (50k+ sampled step-5 rows): per voucher estimate dP/dS beta, up-vs-down asymmetry, spread shock response to top-decile underlying returns, plus BS IV/delta/vega medians under round3 DTE+winding conventions. Used this to build a distinct post-duplicate-check variant with beta-weighted residual ranking and core-vs-wing IV spread regime against EWMA RV.
used_to_decide: Deep propagation findings: strong beta gradient (ITM high-beta/low-vega wings to ATM high-vega lower-beta center), limited spread-shock uplift, and asymmetry concentrated in deep ITM. The distinct family13-adjusted design was too restrictive and effectively produced no incremental edge in worse-fill backtests (v18/v19 total 0). Keep v14 (4666) as branch best despite still far from 800k bar; future attempts should relax gating and avoid over-combining regime and residual filters.

analysis_description: Deep propagation extension: computed contemporaneous and lag-1 betas (dP/dS), delta-normalized elasticity, and per-strike asymmetry on 50k sampled rows. Core finding: strong immediate beta ladder across strikes, but near-zero/negative lag-1 beta for active strikes, and near-zero sensitivity in far OTM wings (K>=6000).
used_to_decide: Given near-zero lag-beta, tested contemporaneous directional gating by dS sign and high-beta strikes (v20/v21). This over-constrained execution and collapsed edge (-36 both). Keep v14 (4666) as best. Next improvement should apply propagation features as soft sizing/width shifts, not hard side gates.

analysis_description: Propagation → execution (soft, not gating): recorded precomputed contemporaneous dP/dS (beta_t) per voucher from underlying_propagation_lag_elasticity.json; built soft_policy table (size multiplier vs beta with conservative clamps) in soft_beta_sizing_policy.json. Same BS IV, quadratic smile residual, and neighbor gating as v14, but sizes scale with beta and v22 also widens the effective neighbor residual cap for low-beta wings (up to +0.09). Filled v22 and ablation v23 (NEIGHBOR_LOOSEN_MAX=0) with full backtest JSON + tradeHistory summaries.
used_to_decide: Soft beta scaling improves total PnL vs v14 by +1 to 4667 under --match-trades worse; hard directional filters (v20/v21) were wrong direction. v23 (no cap widen) matches v22’s PnL, so the gain is from size skew toward high-beta strikes, not the extra neighbor slack; prefer the simpler v23 for ongoing work (or drop NEIGHBOR_LOOSEN_MAX in a future cleanup). Still far below ~800k surface benchmark—next iterations can tune gamma/min/max or add spread-aware soft weights while keeping residual-decile core.

analysis_description: Round-3 price tapes (days 0–2): L1 spread on each VEV vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid moves. One-tick |ΔS| bins are degenerate (median |ΔS|≈1); long-horizon |S(t)-S(t-5000)| quantiles separate calm vs volatile windows. Conditional median L1 spread is nearly flat across those bins on subsampled rows (coarse mids + tight ITM quotes), so spread-shock widening is not a strong linear signal in this aggregation. Implemented soft live sizing anyway: per action tick, haircut size by recent raw-mid step |ΔS|/S and by each voucher’s L1 spread vs tape median (~2 ticks, p90 ~17). BS IV smile residual logic unchanged from v23.
used_to_decide: Tape work showed limited cross-bin spread lift vs large horizon extract moves, so stress sizing stays mild multiplicative (no gates). v24/v25 add move+spread scalers on top of v23; both reach total_profit 4671 (+4 vs v23). v25 grid (softer constants) ties v24; keep v24 as the documented default for slightly stronger defensive skew. Next: optional IV rank using vega-weighted residual noise when spread is wide, or tune MOVE_SHRINK only on high-beta legs.

analysis_description: Round-3 tapes + BS: (1) extract_move_asymmetry_voucher_response.json — signed 1-tick and 5-tick extract changes vs voucher |Δmid| and |Δspread|; ratios up/down are ~0.97–0.99 (no meaningful asymmetry to gate on). (2) theta_rel_by_strike_tape.json — from subsampled mids, bisection IV, T=dte_eff/365; |Θ_day|/mid rises sharply into OTM wings (e.g. VEV_5000 median ~0.006 vs VEV_6500 ~0.75), matching intuition that far OTM decay risk per premium dollar dominates. (3) Backtests v26/v27 add live theta_rel scaler on top of v24.
used_to_decide: No tradeable up/down asymmetry on extract moves; do not add directional filters. Theta/mid ladder is strong and cross-sectionally stable on tape, so v26 applies a soft size haircut where |Θ_day|/mid is high (wings) while keeping residual-decile signal unchanged. v26 and v27 both reach total_profit 4673 (+2 vs v24); v27’s gentler THETA_K ties v26—keep v26 as default reference. Branch best is now v26/v27; still orders of magnitude below the ~800k surface bar.

analysis_description: Read round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt: joint condition spread_5200<=2 AND spread_5300<=2 (L1) aligns with more favorable short-horizon extract mid forward move (K=20) on day-0 tape (Welch t~4.7, P~3e-6). in inclineGod/sonic framing: use spreads as state; P(both tight)~10% on that run. Merged main adds Round 3 day-3 data in Prosperity4Data. Implemented v28 (soft size mult + optional join-bid extract) and v29 (hard: no VEV orders unless gate). Both worse and all backtest logs + tradeHistory fill summaries per user request.
used_to_decide: Soft joint gate (v28) multiplies the existing v27 stack by 1.25 (tight) or 0.45 (wide) and adds a small extract long in tight—total_profit 14390 worse on R3 **days 0–3** (day 3 is new tape). Hard gate (v29) cuts activity too much; total 1160 worse. Next: re-tune WIDE mult vs extract overlay; consider gate-only for the two anchor strikes, not the whole decile; re-run v27 on 4 days for apples-to-apples baseline. Still under 200k/day target.

analysis_description: Deprioritized legacy cross-strike IV-rank thesis per new scope. Re-read round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt (inclineGod: spreads per contract + spread correlation; Sonic: bot flow gated on 5200/5300 both <=2 ticks, t-stat decays when wide; optional forward-mid edge in tight surface from outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt). Implemented standalone traders v30–v32: only when joint L1 spread gate is on, passive join-bid long extract and VEVs (no hydrogel). v31 was a size grid but hit 200/300 position limits so matched v30 PnL; v32 drops other strikes and underperforms v30. Logged worse+all for v30–v32.
used_to_decide: Best in this pass: v30/v31 total 25675 worse over Round 3 days 0–3 (6419/day unweighted average) — still far from 200k/day; simple join-bid longs on tight gate are not enough under worse fills. v32 anchors-only 22973. Next: add exit/flatten logic, maker on both sides in tight, or time-decay/theta-aware rotation; consider crossing when spread=1; possibly repeat gate check only when spreads co-tighten (Sonic 'informed' regime) rather than every tick. Legacy IV-residual line stopped.

analysis_description: Tape add-on gate_on_spread_widths_r3.json: when 5200 and 5300 are both <=2, **conditional** on that event their L1 spreads in Round 3 days 0–3 are always exactly 2.0 (median 2) — the joint gate is almost never 'spread=1' in our tapes. So two-sided 'inner' quotes (bb+1, ba-1) almost never apply on the anchor names when the gate is true; the MM posts at touch. v33/v34 two-sided MM on all VEVs+extract under gate: worse 2594 << v30 25675; v34 size grid matches v33. v35: touch MM extract+5200+5300 only: worse -162, all 2004. Conclusion: for this data, one-sided join-bid v30 still dominates these MM variants; mid-forward edge (STRATEGY) does not convert to PnL with symmetric maker at the touch under worse. inclineGod spread correlation thread preserved by tracking per-name spread in gate logic; Sonic state gate unchanged.
used_to_decide: Keep v30 as best PnL among vouchers_final_strategy runs so far. Next: asymmetric MM (e.g. lean bid in tight per forward-mid story), or single-sided improve with more aggressive size only on 5200/5300 when gate on; avoid full symmetric touch on extract until inventory model. Still nowhere near 200k/day; need more edge than passive quotes.
